{"id":"project-LICHEN-ut5s","title":"Silent discard of out-of-order timestamps","description":"## Location
python/src/lichen/sim/metrics.py:147

## Description
In record_reception(), if time_us < start (negative latency), the latency is silently not recorded. This could indicate a clock bug or race condition in the caller, but no warning is logged. The conditional `time_us >= start` filters these cases, but provides no visibility into potential timing issues.

## Failure Scenario
A bug in the simulation causes record_reception() to be called with a timestamp earlier than the transmission start time. The invalid latency is silently dropped rather than logged, making timing bugs hard to diagnose. Latency statistics appear normal but are actually missing data points.
